title: Pass 5.G.4: ferrotorch-jit autotune.rs O(inputs × values) cache key rebuild on every lookup
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-jit audit, PERF_OBVIOUS.

src/autotune.rs:283-303 — On every `tune` call the cache key is rebuilt by walking topo+nodes+values for every input value (line 271-282 nested O(inputs × values) lookup, 84-95 op-sequence stringification). On a cache hit the entire fingerprint walk runs anyway before the lookup; the supposedly-cheap 'I've seen this' path still pays full key-build cost.

Adjacent finding — codegen_ir.rs:524-545, 548-573, 576-601: every reduction lowering uses scalar acc accumulators via single LoopIR::Store at index 0. No vector/SIMD hint, no parallel-reduction tree even for large numel. Hot-path footgun for sum/mean/prod.

Acceptance:
- Cache the fingerprint on the Graph itself (lazy + invalidated on mutation)
- Add tree-reduction LoopIR for reductions over numel > threshold
- Benchmark: autotune cache hit should not re-walk the graph.
